profile.name: baseline
profile.title: Local Chroma + ONNX MiniLM, current production chunking
profile.description: Reproduces the toolkit's shipped behaviour exactly. Its chunking block repeats the module defaults of index_toolbox.py, so selecting this profile is a no-op with respect to the manifest.

harness.statistics: median, min, max and median absolute deviation always; P50 and P95 only at n >= 100; no P99 at any n

modes.warm: many operations in one process: model loaded, OS cache hot. The four timed operations and the stage split are all warm
modes.cold-start: a fresh subprocess per sample, paying interpreter startup, module import, Chroma client construction and ONNX model load before any work

queries: how does the harvester schedule its lattice passes
quorum drift between the outer and inner relay
what does the ledger say about pumice throughput
cost of a cold restart for the ferrite pipeline

environment.collected: hardware and library versions only; no hostname, user or path

read.unit: ms per pass over 5 fixed queries, k=5

update.unit: ms per re-index of one changed note

delete.unit: ms per removal of one note

consistency.unit: ms until the note stops appearing in results

query_embedding.unit: ms per query, ONNX forward pass

vector_search.unit: ms per query, ANN lookup, include=[]

result_materialization.unit: ms per query, documents+metadatas+distances, as a delta

full_in_process_read.unit: ms per query, full in-process read, k=5

read_stages.materialization_floored_note: materialization is the difference of two measurements and can come out negative on an individual sample; those samples are floored at zero and counted here rather than silently clamped

mcp_roundtrip.unit: ms per tools/call search_toolbox over stdio, k=5
mcp_roundtrip.not_a_summand: the round trip contains a process hop, JSON-RPC framing and the server's own formatting on top of the in-process stages; it is an envelope around them, not one of them

process_wall.unit: ms per fresh process, one pass over 5 fixed queries

init.unit: ms, module import through Chroma client and embedder ready

op.unit: ms, the operation alone, on a ready process

overhead.unit: ms, interpreter startup and teardown (wall - init - op)

process_wall.unit: ms per fresh process, one full index build

init.unit: ms, module import through Chroma client and embedder ready

op.unit: ms, the operation alone, on a ready process

overhead.unit: ms, interpreter startup and teardown (wall - init - op)

quality.relevance: a retrieved chunk is a hit when its source file is in the query's relevant set; labels are never keyed to chunk ids
quality.k_semantics: k counts retrieved chunks (retriever depth); a document is retrieved when any of those chunks has it as its source
quality.measured_after: all four timed ops; excluded from every latency sample

per_query.query: what pressure does the thernwick governor hold at cold start
how often is the thernwick rail re-shimmed
what temperature limit applies to the vashtrin buffer
why does the pellagrove damper need a second anchor
what is the bleed order for the pellagrove manifold
what tool releases the vashtrin escapement
what causes the ganthorpe diaphragm to blister
how is the ganthorpe wickstay tensioned

unanswerable_query.purpose: a query no document answers must score 0
unanswerable_query.query: what clearance does the thernwick escutcheon take when it is refaced

result.query: what clearance does the thernwick escutcheon take when it is refaced

shuffled_ground_truth.purpose: same retrieved results, labels deranged onto document-disjoint sets; the aggregate must drop

per_query.query: what pressure does the thernwick governor hold at cold start
how often is the thernwick rail re-shimmed
what temperature limit applies to the vashtrin buffer
why does the pellagrove damper need a second anchor
what is the bleed order for the pellagrove manifold
what tool releases the vashtrin escapement
what causes the ganthorpe diaphragm to blister
how is the ganthorpe wickstay tensioned

token_budget.measured_after: all timed ops; excluded from every latency sample
token_budget.retrieval_depth_note: min(collection.count(), 128): the budget is meant to bind, not the rank cutoff
token_budget.token_approximation: ceil(len(text) / 4) - a character heuristic, not a tokenizer; applied identically to every profile
token_budget.admission_rule: greedy prefix: walk the ranked chunks in order and admit each while the running total stays within budget, stopping at the first chunk that would exceed it. Not skip-and-continue, which is a different retriever and would flatter small chunks

per_query.query: what pressure does the thernwick governor hold at cold start
how often is the thernwick rail re-shimmed
what temperature limit applies to the vashtrin buffer
why does the pellagrove damper need a second anchor
what is the bleed order for the pellagrove manifold
what tool releases the vashtrin escapement
what causes the ganthorpe diaphragm to blister
how is the ganthorpe wickstay tensioned

control_unanswerable.query: what clearance does the thernwick escutcheon take when it is refaced

per_query.query: what pressure does the thernwick governor hold at cold start
how often is the thernwick rail re-shimmed
what temperature limit applies to the vashtrin buffer

what is the bleed order for the pellagrove manifold

what causes the ganthorpe diaphragm to blister

state_guard.digests_withheld: the tree fingerprints are compared in-process and printed to stdout; they are unsalted hashes of a private directory and are not written to a published report
